title: search-svc latency spike on /search
schema: step-schema.json v2.0 — fully-recorded run (every step kind + situation)

nodes.label: INC-2208
nodes.why_type: kind=incident — the run subject, opened in ServiceNow
nodes.summary: search-svc latency spike from a silently-reclaimed spot compute node → shard rebalance → hot-shard I/O — recovered by adding a node back + throttled rebalance.

facts.value: added a compute node back + throttled shard rebalance; warm-replica read route applied then reversed

nodes.label: search-svc
nodes.summary: Impacted (p99 latency on /search, errors flat); recovered after the node-add + rebalance.

facts.value: flat (~0.1%) — latency-led, not an error storm

nodes.label: search-cluster
nodes.why_type: kind=compute — the Elasticsearch search cluster (CMDB CI class cmdb_ci_compute)
nodes.summary: Root cause — a spot data node (search-data-3) was silently reclaimed @ 09:50 → shards rebalanced onto a hot shard on search-data-1 → query I/O hotspot. Journey ruled_out → degraded → unhealthy → healthy.

facts.value: shard search-idx[2] on search-data-1 — 80% of query time
added search-data-4 + throttled rebalance (1 shard at a time)

nodes.label: catalog-svc
nodes.why_type: kind=service — CMDB CI; a downstream caller of search-svc
nodes.summary: Downstream — calls search-svc on /search; impacted by the latency, recovered with it.

facts.value: calls search-svc on the product-search path
product-page search slow (timeouts at the edge)

nodes.label: rev-310 index-mapping
nodes.why_type: kind=change — a deployment record from GitHub Deployments (an index-mapping change)
nodes.summary: FALSE first suspect — an index-mapping change deployed 09:00; ruled out once the hot shard traced to a reclaimed node, not the mapping.

facts.value: added a keyword sub-field to search-idx mapping (additive, no reindex)
ruled out — onset is 09:50, not 09:00; mapping change is additive

nodes.label: INC-3050
nodes.why_type: kind=incident — a similar prior, fetched in Assess (similarity 0.80)
nodes.summary: Prior: search latency traced to a hot shard after a node loss; resolved by restoring a node + rebalancing.

facts.value: added a node back and rebalanced shards off the hot node

edges.basis: ServiceNow cmdb_rel_ci 'Runs on' — search-svc queries the search-cluster
GitHub: rev-310 index-mapping change deployed to search-svc 09:00
similarity 0.80 — search latency + hot shard after a node loss
80% of query time in one shard; ES shows search-data-3 left 09:50 → shards rebalanced onto a hot shard on search-data-1; aligns with onset

phases.goal: What's affected, how bad, what changed — plus related incidents.
phases.summary: search-svc latency-led (p99 1.8s, errors flat); downstream catalog-svc impacted; the search-cluster is the prime suspect; near-twin prior INC-3050 (hot shard after node loss).

steps.headline: Planned Assess — topology · metrics · priors

plan.why: affected CI + what it runs on + who calls it
similar past incidents to learn from

steps.headline: ServiceNow: search-svc runs on search-cluster; catalog-svc calls it
steps.analysis: search-svc is the affected CI; catalog-svc depends on it; search-svc runs its queries on the search-cluster (Elasticsearch). Topology seeded.

steps.headline: Datadog: search-svc p99 200ms → 1.8s, errors flat — latency-led
steps.analysis: This is latency-led — the error rate is flat, so it's slowness not failure. Onset ~09:50. catalog-svc inherits the slowness. Look below search-svc, at the cluster.

steps.headline: Similar incidents: INC-3050 (0.80) — hot shard after node loss

similar.title: search latency from a hot shard after a node left
similar.resolution: added a node back and rebalanced shards off the hot node

similar.title: search latency from a slow GC pause

steps.analysis: INC-3050 (0.80) is a near-twin — search latency that resolved to a hot shard after a node loss. A strong prior pointing at the cluster's shard distribution, not the app.

steps.headline: Assess finding — latency-led; the search-cluster is prime suspect
steps.body: Affected: search-svc (+ downstream catalog-svc). SEV-2, latency-led (errors flat). The cluster search-svc runs on is the prime suspect; the INC-3050 prior warns the cause may be shard distribution after a node event. A recent change should still be checked first in Root cause.
steps.confidence_basis: symptom shape (latency, not errors) + the INC-3050 prior; nothing measured inside the cluster yet

steps.headline: Checkpoint — proceed to Root cause?
steps.prompt: search-svc latency-led, cluster prime suspect. Proceed to Root cause?

steps.headline: Operator approved — proceed to Root cause
steps.reason: Latency-led with a strong cluster prior — but check the morning's index-mapping deploy before committing to the cluster.

output.impact: SEV-2 — product search slow / timing out at the edge
changed: rev-310 index-mapping deployed 09:00 (to check in Root cause)
suggestions: check the rev-310 mapping change first, then the cluster's shard distribution

phases.goal: The most probable cause — even if it means re-opening a ruled-out node.
phases.summary: rev-310 mapping change led, then a hot-shard trace contradicted a premature 'cluster is fine' ruling; BACKTRACKED to re-open the cluster; ES (after an auth retry) showed search-data-3 left 09:50 → rebalance → hot shard on search-data-1; rev-310 ruled out. Cause = search-cluster (conf 0.88). Operator confirmed a spot-reclaim infra event at 09:50.

steps.headline: Planned Root cause — change diff · quick cluster check · trace · cluster history

plan.why: quick node-CPU sample on the cluster
where is the query time spent?

steps.headline: GitHub: rev-310 index-mapping change @ 09:00 — the lead suspect

output.change: added a keyword sub-field to search-idx mapping (additive)
output.author: p.shah

steps.analysis: One mapping change deployed 09:00 — additive (a new keyword sub-field, no reindex). It's the lead suspect by recency, but additive mappings rarely 9x p99, and it landed ~50 min before onset. Holding it as the lead pending evidence.

evidence.caption: GitHub diff — rev-310 additive mapping change

steps.headline: rev-310 is the lead — but additive mappings rarely spike p99
steps.body: rev-310 is the only change in the window, so it leads. But an additive keyword sub-field with no reindex shouldn't take p99 from 200ms to 1.8s, and the 09:50 onset trails the 09:00 deploy by ~50 min. Suspicious but weak — I'll sample the cluster before committing.

steps.headline: Datadog: quick node-CPU sample on the cluster looked normal
steps.analysis: A single spot-sample of average node CPU read ~41% — looks nominal, so the cluster doesn't look CPU-saturated. (Average across nodes hides a per-shard hotspot — a trap.)

steps.headline: Quick CPU sample looked fine — provisionally ruled out the cluster (premature)
steps.analysis: Average node CPU at 41% looked healthy, so I provisionally ruled the cluster out and leaned back toward rev-310. (This averaged away a per-shard hotspot — a premature ruling I will have to revisit.)

steps.headline: Grafana trace: 80% of query time in one shard — a hot shard
steps.analysis: 80% of query time is spent on ONE shard (search-idx[2] on search-data-1) while the rest are fast. That is a hot-shard I/O hotspot — and it directly contradicts the 'cluster looks fine' average-CPU sample. The cluster must be re-examined.

steps.headline: Re-opened search-cluster — hot-shard I/O contradicts the normal-CPU sample
steps.reason: A single shard holding 80% of query time can't coexist with a 'healthy' cluster — the earlier ruled_out was based on an average-CPU sample that hid the hotspot. Re-opening the cluster and pulling its real node/shard history.

steps.headline: Elasticsearch auth expired — re-authing
steps.analysis: API key expired — refreshing the key and retrying once.

steps.headline: Elasticsearch (retry): search-data-3 left 09:50 → shards rebalanced → hot shard
steps.analysis: search-data-3 left the cluster at 09:50 (node count 4→3). Elasticsearch auto-rebalanced its shards onto the survivors, landing search-idx[2] on search-data-1 alongside its existing shards → an I/O hotspot. The 09:50 node-leave aligns exactly with the app onset. This is the cause; rev-310 is a red herring.

steps.headline: Cause = search-cluster node loss → hot shard (conf 0.88)
steps.body: Chain: search-data-3 left at 09:50 → ES rebalanced its shards → search-idx[2] colocated on search-data-1 → that shard becomes an I/O hotspot (80% of query time) → search-svc p99 spikes → catalog-svc slows. rev-310 ruled out (additive mapping, wrong timing). The cluster is the cause; the app is a victim.
steps.confidence_basis: hot-shard trace (80% on one shard) + ES node-leave at 09:50 matching onset + the INC-3050 prior; re-confirmed after the premature CPU-sample ruling. Held at 0.88 pending the infra cause of the node leave.

steps.headline: Drew the cause edge → search-cluster
steps.analysis: Recording suspected_cause INC-2208 → search-cluster at 0.88. Keeping it suspected (not confirmed) until recovery proves the fix; confirm_cause is written in Verify.

steps.headline: Operator: was there an infra event ~09:50?
steps.body: a node leaving on its own at 09:50 smells like a spot reclaim — can you check the infra event log for ~09:50 before we call it?

steps.headline: infra-events: spot-instance reclaim of search-data-3 @ 09:50 (confirmed)
steps.analysis: Confirmed: search-data-3 was a spot instance reclaimed by the provider at 09:50 (capacity-reclaim) — the silent node leave. That closes the chain: spot reclaim → node leave → rebalance → hot shard. Recording the infra event as a fact on the cluster.

evidence.caption: infra-events — spot interruption of search-data-3 at 09:50

steps.headline: Checkpoint — cause is the cluster node loss; proceed to Remediation?
steps.prompt: Cause = search-cluster node loss (spot reclaim) → hot shard (conf 0.88). Remediation is a cluster action. Proceed?

steps.headline: Operator approved — proceed to Remediation
steps.reason: Spot reclaim confirmed; cause is well-evidenced. The cluster belongs to search-platform — loop them in for the fix.

candidates.cause: search-cluster: spot reclaim of search-data-3 → shard rebalance → hot shard on search-data-1

confidence.basis: hot-shard trace + ES node-leave at 09:50 + confirmed spot reclaim + INC-3050 prior
additive mapping, no reindex; landed 09:00, ~50 min before the 09:50 onset

output.notes: the cluster was ruled out early on an average-CPU sample, then RE-OPENED via backtrack when a hot-shard trace contradicted it.

phases.goal: The safest fix — propose it, gate it; the human approves / refines / denies. The cluster is owned by search-platform, not the app team.
phases.summary: Fix is a cluster action (add a node + rebalance) owned by search-platform — ESCALATED via PagerDuty; first write DENIED (mitigate the read path first); applied a warm-replica read route; RE-PROPOSED the rebalance (supersedes); operator REFINED (throttle, one shard at a time); node-add + throttled rebalance executed.

steps.headline: Planned Remediation — propose the cluster fix, escalate to its owner, gate the write

plan.why: restore a node + rebalance shards off the hot node
the cluster is owned by search-platform, not the app team

steps.headline: The fix restores a node + rebalances — owned by search-platform
steps.body: Recovery means restoring cluster capacity: add a data node back to replace the reclaimed search-data-3, then rebalance search-idx[2] off the hot search-data-1. That is a cluster operation owned by the search-platform team — the app on-call shouldn't run it. We propose it, then escalate.

steps.headline: Proposed: add a node back + rebalance shards (3 options)
steps.proposal: Add a data node back to the search-cluster (replace search-data-3) and rebalance search-idx[2] off the hot search-data-1.

options.label: Do nothing — let load settle
options.risk: SEV-2 persists; hot shard won't self-heal without a node

steps.rationale: Adding a node restores capacity AND gives the rebalancer somewhere to move the hot shard — it fixes the root cause, not just the symptom.
steps.fallback: o2 — force-move the hot shard if a new node can't be provisioned quickly.

steps.headline: Escalated to search-platform on-call (PagerDuty)
steps.reason: Remediation is an Elasticsearch cluster operation (add node + rebalance) — outside app-team authority. Paged search-platform to approve + execute; attached the evidence (search-data-3 spot-reclaimed 09:50, hot shard search-idx[2] on search-data-1).

steps.headline: WRITE gate — approve · refine · deny
steps.prompt: Add node + rebalance is a production cluster write. Approve, refine, or deny?

steps.headline: Operator DENIED — mitigate the read-path impact before any rebalance
steps.reason: Don't kick off a rebalance while users are still hitting the hot shard — the extra shard-movement I/O could make p99 worse before it gets better. Mitigate the read path first (route reads off the hot node), THEN rebalance.

steps.headline: Write denied — mitigate first by routing reads to a warm replica
steps.body: Rebalance denied — nothing applied. The operator is right: a rebalance adds I/O on top of the hot shard. search-idx has a warm replica on search-data-2; routing reads there takes load off the hot primary immediately and makes the subsequent rebalance safe. Proposing that as a temporary mitigation first.

steps.headline: MITIGATION (write): routed search reads to the warm replica
steps.analysis: Read traffic for search-idx now served from the warm replica on search-data-2, off the hot primary on search-data-1. This is a temporary mitigation — it eases the read path so the rebalance can run safely; it must be reversed once the cluster is balanced. Authorized by the operator's deny→mitigate-first instruction.

evidence.caption: Elasticsearch — search-idx reads pinned to the warm replica

steps.headline: Read path mitigated — rebalance is now safe to run
steps.analysis: With reads off the hot primary, the rebalance won't compete with live query I/O. The operator's concern is resolved — re-proposing the node-add + rebalance now that the read path is protected.

steps.headline: Re-proposed: add node + rebalance now that reads are safe
steps.proposal: Now that reads are on the warm replica, add a data node back and rebalance search-idx[2] off search-data-1.

options.label: Add node + rebalance (reads already mitigated)
options.blast_radius: shard-movement I/O — but off the live read path now
options.rollback: drain the new node + lift the read route

steps.rationale: Same root-cause fix, but the operator's objection is now addressed — the read path is protected, so the rebalance I/O no longer threatens p99.

diff.from: Add node + rebalance (reads still on the hot shard — denied)
diff.to: Add node + rebalance AFTER routing reads to the warm replica (read path protected)

steps.headline: WRITE gate (re-raised) — approve · refine · deny
steps.prompt: Read path mitigated. Approve the node-add + rebalance?

steps.headline: Search-platform on-call REFINED — rebalance one shard at a time (throttle)

refinement.add_constraint: throttle: cluster.routing.allocation.node_concurrent_recoveries=1 (one shard at a time) so recovery I/O stays bounded

steps.reason: Approve the direction, but rebalancing all shards at once would saturate the new node's network. Throttle to one concurrent shard recovery — slower, but it won't re-spike the cluster.

steps.headline: WRITE: added search-data-4 + throttled rebalance (1 shard at a time)
steps.analysis: Added search-data-4 and rebalanced with one concurrent recovery per the operator's add_constraint. search-idx[2] moved off search-data-1 onto the fresh node; the cluster is balanced again. Reads are still pinned to the warm replica — that temporary route must be lifted in Verify now that the cluster is healthy.

evidence.caption: Elasticsearch — search-data-4 added; search-idx[2] relocated; cluster balanced

graph_ops.to: search-data-4 added; hot shard relocated; balanced

actions.technique: route search-idx reads to the warm replica on search-data-2
actions.expected_effect: load off the hot primary so the rebalance is safe
actions.rollback: lift the read-preference route (done in Verify)
actions.approval: applied after the operator DENIED rebalance-first and asked to mitigate the read path

actions.technique: add search-data-4 + throttled rebalance (1 shard at a time) off search-data-1
actions.approval: ESCALATED to search-platform (s.okafor) → first DENIED (mitigate reads first) → RE-PROPOSED (supersedes) → REFINED (throttle one shard at a time) → executed

follow_ups: lift the warm-replica read route once the cluster is balanced (Verify)
replace search-data-3 spot instance with an on-demand node

phases.goal: Confirm recovery from the user's side; reverse the temporary mitigation; a human closes; log the open follow-up.
phases.summary: Recovered — p99 1.8s → 210ms within ~2 min of the rebalance; lifted the warm-replica read route (reverses the temp mitigation); closed with one follow-up (replace the spot node with on-demand).

steps.headline: Planned Verify — confirm latency recovered, then lift the read route
Datadog: search-svc p99 1.8s → 210ms (recovered)
steps.analysis: p99 back to baseline within ~2 min of the rebalance completing; the hot shard is gone and query time is evenly distributed. catalog-svc recovered with it. This confirms the node-add + rebalance (action INC-2208-rem-rebalance) resolved the search-cluster cause.

evidence.caption: Datadog — search-svc p99 back to baseline; shard load even

steps.headline: REVERSE (write): lifted the warm-replica read route
steps.note: reverts the temporary warm-replica read route (idem INC-2208-rem-readroute) — inherits the remediation approval; no new write gate needed.
steps.analysis: Temporary read route removed now that the cluster is balanced — search-idx reads are back to the default (primary + replicas across all four nodes). The temporary mitigation is fully reversed; no lingering pin.

steps.headline: Recovered — one open follow-up (replace the spot node with on-demand)
steps.body: search-svc recovered; the hot shard is gone and the cluster is balanced on four nodes. The temporary warm-replica read route is reverted cleanly. Open follow-up: replace the reclaimed spot node (search-data-3) with an on-demand instance so the cluster isn't exposed to another silent reclaim. Ready for a human to close.
steps.confidence_basis: Datadog p99 at baseline + even shard load + the temp read route lifted cleanly

steps.headline: CLOSE gate — a human closes
steps.prompt: Recovered + verified; temporary read route reversed. Close INC-2208? (a spot-node replacement follow-up stays open)

steps.headline: Operator CLOSED INC-2208 (follow-up logged)
steps.reason: Recovered + verified; warm-replica route reversed; spot-node replacement follow-up logged to search-platform. Filed as a prior alongside INC-3050.

output.before_after: search-svc p99 1.8s → 210ms; catalog-svc baseline; hot shard gone
output.resolution: added search-data-4 + throttled rebalance off the hot node; warm-replica read route applied then reversed
temporary_actions_status: warm-replica read route: reverted 10:49 (idem INC-2208-unroute reverses INC-2208-rem-readroute)
follow_ups: replace search-data-3 spot instance with on-demand
